metadata.governingBody: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)

sources.name: Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)

2024-2025.name: Summer Break
2024-2025.description: Longest school holiday period, popular time for family vacations and festivals

2024-2025.name: Winter Break
2024-2025.description: Includes New Year celebrations, one of the most important periods in Japan

2024-2025.name: Spring Break
2024-2025.description: End of academic year break, preparation time for entrance exams and new school year

2025-2026.description: End of academic year break, preparation time for entrance exams and new school year

termStructure.description: Japanese schools follow a three-term system (三学期制)

terms.name: First Term
terms.seasonalNote: Begins with cherry blossom season, symbolizing new beginnings

goldenWeek.name: Golden Week
goldenWeek.description: Series of national holidays from late April to early May
goldenWeek.note: Popular travel period, schools closed for national holidays

obon.name: Obon
obon.description: Traditional period when ancestors' spirits return to Japan
obon.note: Not official school holiday but culturally significant, occurs during summer break

silverWeek.name: Silver Week
silverWeek.description: Occurs when Respect for the Aged Day and Autumnal Equinox Day align for long weekend

regionalSchoolHolidays.note: While MEXT sets the national framework, municipalities (市区町村) can make minor adjustments to dates
variations: Exact start/end dates may vary by 1-3 days between prefectures
Northern regions may have slightly different winter break periods
Some areas may have additional local festival days

internationalSchools.note: International schools in Japan often follow different calendar systems
internationalSchools.commonPattern: Two-semester system with autumn, winter, spring, and summer breaks
internationalSchools.startPattern: Many follow Western patterns starting in August/September
